destinations.name: Kawah Ijen (Ijen Crater)
destinations.location: Banyuwangi / Bondowoso Regency, East Java, Indonesia
destinations.entity_summary: Kawah Ijen is an active volcano at 2,386 m with the world's highest-volume acidic crater lake. Famous for the pre-dawn blue fire sulfuric-gas phenomenon — visible only in suitable weather and gas conditions. Access from Paltuding trailhead via a steep ~3 km night hike. BBKSDA East Java is the regulatory authority.

key_facts.crater_lake: Turquoise, highly acidic (≈ pH 0) — highest-volume acidic crater lake on Earth
key_facts.famous_for: Blue fire phenomenon, acidic lake, sulfur mining
key_facts.access: Night hike from Paltuding trailhead (~3 km, ~90 min ascent)

blue_fire.approved_language: Blue Fire is a natural phenomenon subject to weather and gas activity.

health_screening.gate_rule: No Valid QR Code = No Crater Zone Access

destinations.name: Mount Bromo (Gunung Bromo)
destinations.location: Tengger Caldera, Probolinggo Regency, East Java
destinations.entity_summary: Mount Bromo is an active volcano in the Tengger caldera, East Java. Best known for the Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint over the sea-of-sand caldera floor and the active smoking crater. Accessed by 4WD jeep from Cemoro Lawang in the pre-dawn window (03:00–05:00).

key_facts.national_park: Bromo Tengger Semeru — BBKSDA permit required
key_facts.famous_for: Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint (2,770 m), Tengger caldera sea of sand, active smoking crater
key_facts.access: 4WD jeep from Cemoro Lawang — mandatory (no private vehicles in caldera zone)
key_facts.entrance_fee: IDR 160,000/person for foreign tourists (JVTO handles; guests do not pay separately)

health_screening.note: Unlike Ijen, Bromo does not have a BBKSDA health-certificate requirement.

destinations.name: Madakaripura Waterfall (Air Terjun Madakaripura)
destinations.type: Horseshoe curtain waterfall in narrow canyon

height_m.note: Main curtain ~100 m (SSOT §9_4). Total incl. upper tiers ~200 m (indonesia.travel + multiple independent sources).

destinations.entity_summary: Madakaripura is Java's tallest waterfall in Probolinggo, East Java. A narrow canyon gorge leads to a horseshoe curtain waterfall fed from multiple directions. Historical context: said to be the final meditation site of Gajah Mada, Majapahit prime minister. Easy ~20 min trek from roadside parking.

key_facts.historical_context: Final meditation site of Gajah Mada, Majapahit prime minister (14th century)
key_facts.access: Easy ~20 min trek from roadside parking (~1.5 km canyon walk); horse option available
key_facts.visitor_note: Visitors will get wet — raincoat strongly recommended. Narrow canyon approach, wet stones.
key_facts.helmet_note: Helmets provided by local site management (NOT a JVTO inclusion)

destinations.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall (Air Terjun Tumpak Sewu)
destinations.entity_summary: Tumpak Sewu is a multi-tiered canyon waterfall (~120 m) in Lumajang, East Java. Nicknamed 'Java's Niagara' for its curtain of streams cascading over a curved cliff face into a canyon. Accessed via rim viewpoint or steep canyon descent (~300 steps).

access_options: Rim viewpoint — panoramic, short walk from parking, suitable for all fitness levels
Base canyon descent — ~300 steps, ropes in places, moderate-challenging fitness
key_facts.guide_note: JVTO guides assess fitness on the day and brief the descent before starting.

destinations.name: Papuma Beach (Pantai Papuma)
destinations.type: White-sand beach with rocky cape (Tanjung Papuma)
destinations.entity_summary: Papuma Beach in Jember offers white sand and dramatic coastal rock formations. JVTO includes it as a coastal contrast stop on Papuma-family packages (4D+). Best for guests who want a beach break on an otherwise volcano-focused East Java itinerary.

key_facts.role: Coastal break between Ijen (2,386 m) and Tumpak Sewu legs — only coastal destination on JVTO East Java circuits
features: Cape headland walk rising to ~86 m
key_facts.note: All packages including Papuma also include Ijen. Papuma comes after the Ijen night hike.
